title: Bacterial Cryopreservation
author: Dr. Steven Wilhelm
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ihncb5e

steps.title: Take a dense culture and transfer...
steps.instruction: Take a dense culture and transfer a volume of cells (usually 20 mL) into a sterile centrifuge tube

steps.title: Spin down the culture at 4,500 rpm, 10 min 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Spin down the culture at 4,500 rpm, 10 min 00:10:00

steps.title: Resuspend pellet into 2 mL of the appropriate growth medium
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet into 2 mL of the appropriate growth medium

steps.title: Split the 2 mL into two...
steps.instruction: Split the 2 mL into two cryogenic vials by adding 1 mL to each vial

steps.title: Remove cryogenic vials one at a time from the freezer
steps.instruction: Remove cryogenic vials one at a time from the freezer

steps.title: Using a sterile pipette tip, scrape...
steps.instruction: Using a sterile pipette tip, scrape a small amount of frozen cells and place into 20 mL sterile growth medium. Slowly move the cells to the appopriate growth conditions. undetermined

steps.title: Place cryogenic vial immediately back into the freezer
steps.instruction: Place cryogenic vial immediately back into the freezer
